top_signals.title: Stocks Fall as AI Spending Worries Grow, Oil Rises: Markets Wrap
top_signals.summary: Stocks fell as unease over ever-higher artificial intelligence spending outweighed an otherwise strong earnings report from Alphabet Inc., while crude rose for a fifth straight day and pushed bond yields higher.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Ford hopes Spain tie-up with China’s Geely will revive European business
top_signals.summary: Two carmakers say they will form a manufacturing joint venture at US group’s Valencia plant
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: EasyJet Says Customers are Resuming More Normal Booking Patterns
top_signals.summary: EasyJet Plc said customers are becoming more confident to book longer in advance again after resorting to last-minute purchases out of concern of cancelations, helping the low-cost carrier improve predictability and steady operations.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: A stealth fighter jet could blow a hole in Healey’s budget
top_signals.summary: To fly, Tempest and the GCAP need to defy the history of failed joint national projects
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Burnham’s high-risk start may help him get ahead of political pain
top_signals.summary: PM’s bet on time-consuming Whitehall reform could mean he is building up the case to seek a mandate of his own
